---
title: "Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures"
---

## Learning objectives

- Contrast **statistical** (start with a model you can write down, emphasize uncertainty) and **algorithmic** (start with flexible prediction, still check errors honestly) viewpoints — good practice borrows from both.
- Explain **decision trees** as a chain of **if–then** rules: each split asks a yes/no question about one predictor; depth and minimum leaf size control how detailed the tree may get.
- Connect the **tree picture** (rectangular regions in “measurement space”) to what you see in an **`rpart.plot`** for penguins.

## Key messages

- Trees are a **bridge**: interpretable splits today, ensembles and boosting tomorrow.
- The same **Palmer Penguins** table supports **logistic regression** (Monday feel) and **trees plus Tuesday’s full “fit and tune in R” walk-through** (Module 04).

## Concept outline

### Two cultures (compressed)

- Different priorities on **writing the model first** vs **minimizing prediction error first**; both care about **leakage** and **fair evaluation**.

### Tree mechanics

- Greedy binary splits; Gini / entropy; stopping vs pruning.
- **Interactions** show up automatically: a path of splits is already a combination of predictors — you do not have to type `x1:x2` by hand.

### When trees shine or struggle

- Shine: nonlinear boundaries, mixed types, monotone-ish effects without manual basis expansion.
- Struggle: very high noise, tiny *n* without regularization via depth / `min_n` / ensembles.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Tuesday — Trees and pipelines](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)
- **Notebooks — penguins:** [Adelie vs Gentoo](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd) (`rpart` + logistic boundary sketch), [three species](../notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd), [sex](../notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd)
- **Data:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
- **Next module:** [Module 04 — Modeling pipeline in R](module-04-pipeline.qmd) (full tuned-tree example on penguins)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- One **hand-drawn style** figure of 2D partition (optional asset) or link to whiteboard photo policy from instructors.
- Cross-link to Tuesday slides on **comparing many models fairly** (`workflowsets` / `stacks` in the code) when students are ready for a capstone.
:::
